headline: Regeneron Shares Up on Trump Drug Pricing Deal
summary: Regeneron, in a press release, said it would get tariff relief for three years under terms with the U.S. Department of Commerce.

headline: Novo Nordisk Expands GLP-1 Reach With Pediatric Oral Semaglutide Data
summary: Novo Nordisk (CPSE:NOVO B) reported strong phase 3 trial results for its oral semaglutide in children and adolescents with type 2 diabetes. The company plans to file for US and EU label expansions to include this pediatric population, using the oral version of its GLP-1 therapy already sold as Rybelsus. The data extend Novo Nordisk’s GLP-1 franchise into youth diabetes care as competitors work on alternative dosing options for similar drugs. Novo Nordisk, known for GLP-1 treatments such as...

headline: Want $4,200 in Passive Income? Invest $90,000 ($30,000 Each) Into These 3 High-Yield Dividend Stocks
summary: Market volatility has rattled portfolios in 2026, with broad equity indexes absorbing sharp swings tied to tariff uncertainty and shifting rate expectations. For investors who have watched their growth positions whipsaw, the appeal of dividend income has never been sharper. A stock that pays you $1,200 a year does so whether the market is up ... headline: Helus Pharma Strengthens Scientific Advisory Board with Addition of Dr. Robert Langer and Dr. Stephen Brannan

headline: Pfizer’s Monthly GLP-1 Push Tests Balance Between Obesity Growth And Dividends
summary: Pfizer (NYSE:PFE) reported Phase 2b results for its GLP-1 anti-obesity candidate, PF-08653944, showing potential for a once monthly injection. The company plans more than 20 anti-obesity trials, including 10 Phase 3 studies targeted for 2026, as it builds out its obesity franchise. This GLP-1 program is aimed at competing with current obesity drug leaders that offer primarily weekly dosing options. For investors following NYSE:PFE, this move into obesity treatments adds a new angle to a...

headline: Richest countries in 2026: New measure of wealth pushes France and Germany out of top ten
summary: Measures of “richest countries” can be misleading. A new prosperity index — looking at income, GDP and how wealth translates into quality of life, social cohesion and long-term development — does not place the US, Germany, or France in the top ten.View on euronews
